# Tasks — 2026-05-30

**Input**: `.github/research/2026-05-30-plan.md`
**Tracking issue**: [#578](https://github.com/ausardcompany/alexi/issues/578) — `[tool] Walk parent directories for nearby AGENTS.md and inject as <system-reminder> after file reads`
**Source research**: `.github/research/2026-05-30-research.md`

## Scope

Only **one** new feature is in scope this cycle: per-directory `AGENTS.md` injection
as `<system-reminder>` after `read` tool calls. (Plan items #2 and #3 are already
filed as `#530` and `#532` and have their own implementation tracks; items #4–#6
plus the deferred POSIX audit are already shipped — see plan summary table.)

Estimate from plan: medium (~1 day). All file paths below are repo-relative.

## Format: `[ID] [P?] Description`

- **[P]** = can be implemented in parallel with other `[P]` tasks (different files,
  no shared mutable state)
- Sequential (no `[P]`) tasks must land in the listed order — they share files or
  depend on a typing change made earlier in the list.
- Each task lists the **exact files** it touches and is small enough to be one
  reviewable commit.

---

## Phase 1: Type plumbing (blocks everything else)

**Purpose**: Add the `systemReminders` channel to `ToolResult.metadata` and the
optional `agentsMdSeen` field on `ToolContext` so downstream code can compile
against a stable shape.

- [ ] **T001** Extend `ToolResult.metadata` typing in `src/tool/index.ts`:
  document `systemReminders?: Array<{ source: string; content: string }>` while
  preserving the existing `[k: string]: unknown` index signature so any
  pre-existing `metadata` writers still typecheck. No runtime behaviour change.
- [ ] **T002** Add `agentsMdSeen?: Set<string>` to the `ToolContext` interface
  in `src/tool/index.ts`. Document that the orchestrator owns its lifetime
  (one set per `execute` call) and tools must treat it as opaque.

**Checkpoint**: `npm run typecheck` passes; no test changes yet.

---

## Phase 2: Pure walker helper (parallelisable with Phase 1 once T001/T002 land)

**Purpose**: Add the directory walker as an exported pure helper, fully
unit-tested. No callers wired up yet — this phase is a self-contained module.

- [ ] **T003** Implement `instructionsForPath(absPath, workdir, seen)` in
  `src/agent/system.ts`. Behaviour:
  - Walks `path.dirname(absPath)` upward, **excluding** `workdir` itself
    (already covered by `loadInstructionFiles`).
  - At each step looks for `AGENTS.md`; if present, resolves via `safeRealpath`
    and skips when the resolved path is already in `seen`; otherwise adds to
    `seen` and pushes `{ path, content }`.
  - Order: nearest-first.
  - Refuses paths outside `workdir` (`path.relative(workdir, absPath)` starts
    with `..`) → returns `[]`.
  - Cap walk depth at 32 to defend against pathological symlink layouts
    (mirrors cycle-protection in `src/skill/index.ts`).
  - Re-uses existing `loadTextFile` and `safeRealpath` from `system.ts`. No
    new I/O primitives.
- [ ] **T004** [P] Unit tests in `src/agent/system.test.ts` (extend existing
  file). Cases:
  1. Returns `[]` when no `AGENTS.md` exists between `workdir` and the file.
  2. Returns reminders for `apps/api/AGENTS.md` when reading
     `apps/api/src/index.ts` from a `workdir` of the repo root.
  3. De-dupes when the same file is read twice with a shared `seen` set.
  4. Refuses paths outside `workdir`.
  5. Symlinked `AGENTS.md` resolves via `realpath` and de-dupes against a
     sibling that points to the same target (mirrors
     `src/skill/index.ts:223-253`).
  6. (Optional but recommended) walk-depth cap — synthetic 33-deep tree
     returns at most 32 reminders.

**Checkpoint**: `npm test -- src/agent/system.test.ts` passes. Helper is
exported but not yet used.

---

## Phase 3: Wire helper into the `read` tool

**Purpose**: Make `read` emit `metadata.systemReminders` for newly seen
`AGENTS.md` files between `workdir` and the read file.

- [ ] **T005** Modify `src/tool/tools/read.ts`:
  - After a successful read, call
    `instructionsForPath(absPath, context.workdir, context.agentsMdSeen ?? new Set())`.
  - For any non-empty result, set
    `result.metadata.systemReminders = items.map(({ path, content }) => ({ source: path, content }))`.
  - When `context.agentsMdSeen` is `undefined`, the tool MUST still function
    (just no de-dupe across calls). This keeps direct-test usage of `read`
    unaffected.
- [ ] **T006** [P] Integration test in
  `src/tool/tools/__tests__/read.agentsmd.test.ts` (new file). Cases:
  1. Read tool returns `metadata.systemReminders` populated when an
     intermediate `AGENTS.md` exists between `workdir` and the file.
  2. `metadata.systemReminders` is empty/absent when no intermediate
     `AGENTS.md` exists.
  3. Honors a per-context `agentsMdSeen` so a second read of a sibling file
     does not re-emit the same reminder.
  - Use the existing `fs.mkdtemp` + `afterEach` cleanup pattern documented
    in `AGENTS.md` ("Tool tests typically create a temp `workdir`...").

**Checkpoint**: `npm test -- src/tool/tools` passes; the new integration test
exercises the full read → metadata path.

---

## Phase 4: Orchestrator wiring (must follow Phase 3)

**Purpose**: Allocate the per-execute seen-set in both orchestrators and emit
one synthetic `role: 'user'` `<system-reminder>` message per tool call when
new reminders are present.

- [ ] **T007** Modify `src/core/agenticChat.ts`:
  - Around the `toolContext` construction site (~`agenticChat.ts:493`),
    allocate `const agentsMdSeen = new Set<string>()` once per execute and
    pass it into `toolContext`.
  - In the post-tool-call block (`agenticChat.ts:633-639`), after pushing
    the `role: 'tool'` message, if
    `toolResult.metadata?.systemReminders?.length`, push **one** synthetic
    `role: 'user'` message whose content is the joined reminders, each
    wrapped in `<system-reminder source="…">…</system-reminder>` blocks.
    Match the in-tree convention already used by hooks
    (`agenticChat.ts:656-659`).
- [ ] **T008** [P] Mirror the change in `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts`:
  same per-execute `agentsMdSeen` allocation + the same synthetic
  `role: 'user'` reminder emission at the equivalent post-tool-call hook
  point.
- [ ] **T009** Orchestrator-level test (extend the closest existing
  `tests/orchestrator.test.ts` or `src/core/*.test.ts`): assert that when a
  mock `read` returns `metadata.systemReminders`, the next message in the
  conversation history is a `role: 'user'` message containing the wrapped
  `<system-reminder source="…">` block(s). One test per orchestrator is
  enough — keep them parallel-shaped so future changes apply uniformly.

**Checkpoint**: `npm test` passes end-to-end. Manual smoke: `npm run dev --
chat -m "read apps/api/src/index.ts"` in a repo with a per-directory
`AGENTS.md` shows the reminder injected on the next turn.

---

## Phase 5: Verification

**Purpose**: Match the local CI order from `AGENTS.md`. No new code; just
gates.

- [ ] **T010** Run the project's pre-push gate locally in CI order:
  `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run format:check && npm run test:coverage && npm run build`.
- [ ] **T011** Confirm coverage on the new code path clears the CI 40 % lines
  floor (`coverage/coverage-summary.json`). Helper + read-tool integration
  tests should be sufficient — if not, add a small targeted test in the
  same file rather than padding.
- [ ] **T012** Open the PR; reference issue `#578` in the description. Use a
  Conventional-Commits message with one of the allowed scopes from
  `commitlint.config.cjs` — recommended `feat(tools): …` or
  `feat(agent): …` (not `feat(agents): …`, that scope is not in the
  enum).

---

## Dependency graph

```
T001 ──┐
       ├──► T003 ──► T004 (P)
T002 ──┘            │
                    ├──► T005 ──► T006 (P)
                    │             │
                    │             ├──► T007 ──► T009
                    │             └──► T008 (P) ──► T009
                    │
                    └──► T010 → T011 → T012
```

T001 and T002 can land in the same commit (both edit `src/tool/index.ts`).
T004, T006, T008 are flagged `[P]` because they live in different files from
their pre-requisites and only read the helper / metadata shape.

## Out of scope (next-cycle candidates from plan §"Notes for the next research cycle")

- `/memory list` enhancement to surface per-directory `AGENTS.md` files an
  agent has picked up during a session.
- Re-baselining against claude-code v2.1.158+ and kilocode 7.4.x.
- SAP-AI-Core auto-mode classifier availability, OTEL instrumentation
  demand, dynamic-workflows feasibility under single-binary CLI constraints.
